body:value: The data refers to the Pattern of Electricity Consumption (Utilities) in India. It gives the percentage share of electricity consumption in each Utility area (Domestic, Commercial, Industry, Traction, Agriculture and others) for various years.

field_group_name:name: Economic Survey 2012-13 - Industry and Infrastructure
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Finance
Department of Economic Affairs

Economy
title: Pattern of Electricity Consumption (Utilities)

body:value: The data refers to the percentage distribution of awh by level of education. It gives state-wise details regarding the percentage of Anganwasi helpers who are illiterate, below primary, primary, middle school, matric/high school and high school & above.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage Distribution of AWH by Level of Education

body:value: The data refers to the percentage distribution of (Anganwadi Helpers) AWH by residence. It is observed that on an average 88.6 per cent of AWHs were residing in the village itself while 9.2 per cent and 1.2 per cent were from other villages within 5 Km and more than 5Km respectively. In almost all the states, the majority of the AWWs live in the village, with the figures ranging between 67.2 and 100 per cent.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage Distribution of AWH by Residence

body:value: The data refers to the Percentage distribution of AWW by level of education. It is observed that on an average 1.8 per cent of AWWs were illiterate, 0.4 per cent, 4.6 per cent and 14.2 per cent had obtained below primary, primary and middle level education respectively.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage distribution of AWW by level of education

body:value: The data refers to details on percentage distribution of rural and urban households by source of drinking water such as tap, well, tubewell/handpump, tank/pond, river, canal and lake, springs and others for 2001 and 2011.

field_group_name:name: Housing
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
field_sector:name: Housing
Statistics
title: Percentage Distribution Of Rural And Urban Households By Source Of Drinking Water

body:value:
The data refers to that the importance of educational empowerment assumes special importance in the context of minorities, especially Muslims, who have
been lagging behind the rest. The data refers to Percentage Distribution of Workers by Category based on religion.

field_sector:name: Social Development
Minority
title: Percentage Distribution Of Workers By Category Based On Religion

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC that have been beneficial to the community. It provides the community leaders perception about benefit of AWC in the village. About 70 per cent of community leaders felt that the ICDS programme was very beneficial to the community and 26.5 per cent of them regarded the programme to be beneficial to some extent in the country.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC beneficial to the community

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC having adequacy of space for different type of activities. It gives state-wise details of AWCs space for carrying out various ICDS activities such as Cooking, Storage and Indoor Activities.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC have adequacy of space for different type of activities

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC having functional weighing scales and other inventories. The data refers to state-wise details regarding availability of Weighing Scales, Utensils, Cooking Vessels, Almirahs, Toys, Posters, etc at AWCs.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC having functional weighing scales and other inventories

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC having a source of drinking water. The data refers to state-wise details regarding the AWCs equipped with piped water, hand pump water or other.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC having source of drinking water

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC having a source of drinking water according to distance. The data refers to state-wise details regarding the AWCs equipped with drinking water within the premises, up to 50 M, greater than 50 meters distance.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC having source of drinking water according to distance

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC having toilet facilities. The data refers to state-wise details regarding availability of toilet facilities, Bihar, UP and Orissa fared the worst. It has a direct bearing on the surrounding environment of AWC in these states and serves as a critical indicator for assessing their sanitation status.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC having toilet Facilities

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC having type of housing facility. It gives state-wise details of AWCs using Owned, Rented, Primary School or Other facility to conduct their activities.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC having type of housing facility

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC provided types of services reported by AWW. The data refers to state-wise details regarding the services that the AWC is supposed to provide under the KSY scheme which includes Food/Supplementary Nutrition, Health check-up, Immunisation, Referral services, De-worming tablets, Iron & folic acid and Family-life education.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC provided types of services reported by AWW

body:value: The data refers to the Percentage of AWC providing different types of services which are reported by the AWW. The services provided at the AWC as part of the ICDS include Supplementary Nutrition, Immunisation, Health Check-ups, Referral Services, Pre-School Education, Nutrition & Health Education and Kishori Shakti Yojana.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC providing different types of services - Reported by the AWW

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWC which received medical kit. It gives state-wise details on the number of AWCs which have received medical kits or not.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC received medical kit

body:value: The data refers to the Percentage of AWC reported the availability and utilisation of Medicine during last one year. It gives state-wise details regarding various types of medicines received and utilized by AWC beneficiaries.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC reported Availability and Utilisation of Medicine

body:value: The data refers to the Percentage of AWC trained adequately to conduct PSE. The Pre-School Education Component (PSE) of the ICDS focuses on overall development of children in the 3-6 years of age brackets, The data refers to details on whether or not workers in AWC have adequate training to conduct them.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWC trained adequately to conduct PSE

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of AWW attended training and duration (in days). It gives details regarding job training, refresher course in growth monitoring, refresher course in pre-school education, joint training with health worker and skill training/special training.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of AWW Attended Training and Duration (In Days)

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of children (aged below 6 year) suffered from the most common diseases during last one year. It gives the Community leaders perception about common diseases of children in the village. Rajasthan has the highest percentage of children (79.2%) free from all diseases, followed by Punjab (75.4%) as reported by the leaders.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of children (aged below 6 year) suffered from the most common diseases

body:value: The data refers to the percentage of community leader helps in AWC activities. It gives state-wise details of whether the community leaders have reported their help towards AWCs. On the national average, more than 50 per cent community leaders admitted that they did not contribute to the functioning of AWC.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Percentage of community leader helps in AWC activities
